tools.comment: Reads the complete contents of a file as text, optionally limited to the first or last N lines (deprecated in favor of read_text_file).
Reads the complete contents of a text file, optionally limited to the first or last N lines.
Reads an image or audio file and returns its base64-encoded data and MIME type.
Reads the contents of multiple files simultaneously, returning each file's content keyed by its path.
Creates a new file or completely overwrites an existing file with the provided text content.
Makes line-based edits to a text file by replacing exact line sequences, returning a git-style diff of the changes.
Creates a new directory (including nested directories) at the specified path, succeeding silently if it already exists.
Returns a detailed listing of all files and directories within the specified path, distinguishing files from directories.
Returns a detailed listing of all files and directories within the specified path including their sizes, with optional sorting.
Returns a recursive JSON tree view of all files and directories under the specified path, with optional exclusion patterns.
Moves or renames a file or directory from a source path to a destination path in a single operation.
Recursively searches for files and directories matching a glob-style pattern within the specified path, returning full matching paths.
Retrieves detailed metadata (size, timestamps, permissions, type) about a file or directory without reading its content.
Returns the list of directories that the server is permitted to access.
Stages specified files or all changes in the repository for the next commit.
Shows line-by-line authorship information for a file, revealing who last modified each line and when.
Manages git branches by listing, creating, deleting, renaming, or showing the current branch.
Gathers git history context and structured review instructions to support LLM-driven changelog analysis.
Switches branches, creates new branches, or restores specific working tree files to a given state.
Applies specific commits from other branches onto the current branch without merging entire branches.
Removes untracked files (and optionally directories) from the working directory, with a dry-run preview option.
Clears the session working directory setting so subsequent git operations require an explicit path parameter.
Clones a remote git repository to a local path, with support for shallow cloning and bare/mirror modes.
Creates a new commit recording staged changes in the repository with a specified commit message.
Shows differences between commits, branches, staged changes, or the working tree in unified diff format.
Downloads objects and refs from a remote repository without merging them into the local branch.
Initializes a new Git repository at the specified path, creating a .git directory and setting up the initial branch.
Displays commit history with optional filtering by author, date, file path, or commit message pattern.
Merges another branch into the current branch using optional merge strategies and commit options.
Fetches changes from a remote repository and integrates them into the current local branch.
Uploads local commits to a remote repository branch, with options for force push, tagging, and branch deletion.
Reapplies commits on top of another base tip to produce a cleaner linear history, with support for continue/abort/skip modes.
Displays the reference logs tracking when branch tips and other git references were updated, useful for recovering lost commits.
Manages remote repository configurations by listing, adding, removing, renaming, or updating remote URLs.
Resets the current HEAD to a specified state, with modes ranging from unstaging files to discarding all changes.
Sets the session working directory so subsequent git commands can omit the path parameter.
Displays details of a git object (commit, tree, blob, or tag) including commit metadata and diffs.
Manages stashes by listing, saving, restoring, or removing stashed working directory changes.
Shows the current working tree status including staged, unstaged, and untracked files.
Manages git tags by listing all tags, creating new lightweight or annotated tags, or deleting existing tags.
Manages multiple git working trees by listing, adding, removing, moving, or pruning worktrees.
Returns the user's desired git wrap-up workflow instructions and current repository status to guide committing and documenting changes.
Fetches the content of a given URL via HTTP GET and returns it in the specified format.
Searches the web using a configured search provider and returns a list of results for a given query.
Adds a review comment to the requester's existing pending pull request review.
Adds a comment to a specific issue or pull request in a GitHub repository.
Adds a reply to an existing pull request comment, linking it as a threaded response.
Assigns GitHub Copilot as an agent to work on a specific issue, potentially creating a pull request with code changes.
Creates a new branch in a GitHub repository, optionally from a specified source branch.
Creates or updates a single file in a GitHub repository with a commit message.
Creates a new pull request in a GitHub repository between specified head and base branches.
Creates a new GitHub repository in the authenticated user's account or a specified organization.
Deletes a file from a GitHub repository on a specified branch with a commit message.
Forks a GitHub repository to the authenticated user's account or a specified organization.
Retrieves details for a specific commit from a GitHub repository, optionally including diffs.
Retrieves the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository at an optional ref or SHA.
Retrieves details of a specific label from a GitHub repository.
Retrieves the latest release from a GitHub repository.
Retrieves profile details of the currently authenticated GitHub user.
Retrieves a specific release from a GitHub repository identified by its tag name.
Retrieves details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository.
Retrieves the member usernames of a specific team within a GitHub organization.
Retrieves the teams that a specified user (or the authenticated user) is a member of.
Reads information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository, including details, comments, sub-issues, or labels.
Creates a new issue or updates an existing issue in a GitHub repository.
Lists branches in a GitHub repository with pagination support.
Lists commits on a branch or up to a specific SHA in a GitHub repository.
Lists the supported issue types configured for a GitHub organization.
Lists issues in a GitHub repository with filtering and pagination support.
Lists pull requests in a GitHub repository with filtering and pagination support.
Lists releases in a GitHub repository with pagination support.
Lists git tags in a GitHub repository with pagination support.
Merges a pull request in a GitHub repository using a specified merge method.
Retrieves information about a specific pull request, including details, diffs, status, files, review comments, reviews, or comments.
Creates, submits, or deletes a pull request review on a GitHub repository.
Pushes multiple files to a GitHub repository in a single commit on a specified branch.
Requests an automated GitHub Copilot code review for a specified pull request.
Searches for code across GitHub repositories using GitHub's native code search syntax.
Searches for issues across GitHub repositories using GitHub's issue search syntax.
Searches for pull requests across GitHub repositories using GitHub's pull request search syntax.
Searches for GitHub repositories by name, description, topics, or other metadata.
Searches for GitHub users by username, real name, or other profile information.
Adds, removes, or reprioritizes a sub-issue under a parent issue in a GitHub repository.
Updates an existing pull request's title, description, state, base branch, reviewers, or draft status.
Updates a pull request's branch with the latest changes from its base branch.
